name: Anna Schreiber
role: East Berlin university student, Humboldt University, second year
publicFace: A young woman in a dark wool coat, her face flushed from cold and exertion, who has positioned herself at the front of the crowd with a calm that the people behind her mistake for courage
privateGoal: To reach her mother, who crossed to West Berlin on a day visa three weeks ago and has been unable to return — Anna has had no contact since and does not know if she is alive
fear: That she will do something — shout the wrong word, move at the wrong moment — that triggers the guard across the turnstile into firing, and that the deaths will be her fault
knowledge: She has been attending Monday demonstrations in Leipzig since October and has developed an instinct for crowd psychology — she can feel a surge building before it starts
The guard directly opposite her, a young man of perhaps twenty, has been making eye contact with her for the past twenty minutes — she believes he is as frightened as she is
Her mother left a message with a neighbor three days ago: she is staying at an address in Prenzlauer Berg on the western side — Anna has the address memorized
voice: Direct and physical — speaks in short declarative sentences, uses her body as punctuation, rarely raises her voice because she knows the crowd will amplify whatever she projects
trustLogic: Trusts anyone who demonstrates they are thinking about the people around them rather than themselves; immediately suspicious of anyone who seems to be performing bravery for an audience
secrets: She is three months pregnant — the father is a fellow student who does not know, and she has told no one; the physical compression at the front of the crowd is genuinely dangerous for her
She was a registered informant for the Stasi for eight months in her first year at university — she reported nothing of consequence, but the file exists and she lives in terror of it surfacing

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Asks a clarifying question — who told you that, when did that happen — buying time while she assesses
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Turns to face whoever is pressuring her so that the crowd can see the exchange; makes the confrontation public, which usually de-escalates it
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: She will never acknowledge the Stasi informant file — she has constructed an internal narrative in which those eight months did not happen
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the crowd surges and she loses her footing — her survival instinct will override her position at the front
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Sideways — she has identified two gaps in the crowd that lead back toward the Trabant queue
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Does not run; uses the crowd as cover, moving with the flow rather than against it
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Goes completely quiet and cooperative — she knows what Stasi detention looks like and will not give them a reason to escalate
